title: Lethal Hits
teaches: a critical hit — a natural 6 to hit — can do more than hit: Lethal Hits skips the wound roll entirely, though the target still gets its saves.
intro: Your Necron Warriors raise their gauss flayers against a squad of Space Marines. These weapons carry LETHAL HITS: whenever a hit die lands a natural 6 — a critical hit — that attack wounds automatically, skipping the Strength-versus-Toughness roll. Against Toughness 4 Marines your Strength 4 beams would normally need 4+ to wound, so every critical is a coin-flip you don't have to make. Watch the HIT line for sixes, then see them reappear on the WOUND line as automatic wounds.

turns.narrate_before: Fire the first volley. Any natural 6 to hit becomes an automatic wound — the WOUND line will show them added on top of the rolled wounds.
Fire again — and notice the Marines' 3+ armour still applies to every wound, automatic or not. Lethal Hits skips one roll, not all of them.

outro: That is your first weapon ability. A natural 6 to hit is a critical hit, and Lethal Hits converts each one straight into a wound — no Strength-versus-Toughness roll. But an auto-wound is not an auto-kill: the Marines' 3+ armour saved as normal, because Lethal Hits skips exactly one step of the sequence. Other abilities feed on the same critical trigger — ask 'why hit' to read about them.
